---
title: Shortcodes
---

::: {.callout-tip}
Click the `</>` button in the top navigation bar to view the original `.qmd` source code.
:::

Shortcodes are inline directives written as `{{< name args >}}`. They are processed during rendering and replaced with format-aware output. *Calepin* provides several built-in shortcodes and supports user-defined shortcodes via plugins.


# Syntax

Shortcodes use the following syntax: `{{< name arg1 arg2 key="value" >}}`

- Positional arguments are space-separated tokens after the shortcode name.
- Keyword arguments use `key=value` or `key="quoted value"` syntax.

To show a shortcode literally without processing it, wrap it in triple braces. For example, `{{< meta title >}}` in your source renders as the literal text `{{< meta title >}}` without being evaluated.


# Built-in shortcodes

## pagebreak

Insert a native page break. Produces format-appropriate output: `<div style="page-break-after: always;">` in HTML, `\newpage{}` in LaTeX, `#pagebreak()` in Typst.

Page one content.

`{{< pagebreak >}}`

Page two content.

## meta

Print a value from the document's YAML front matter, such as: `{{< meta title >}}`.

The title of this document is: {{< meta title >}}.

This works with standard fields (`title`, `subtitle`, `author`, `date`, `abstract`) and custom metadata.

## env

Print a system environment variable, such as: `{{< env SHELL >}}`. If the variable is not set, the shortcode produces an empty string.

The `SHELL` environment variable on the local machine is: {{< env SHELL >}}.


## var

Look up a variable from `_variables.yml` using dot-notation. For example, this project's `_variables.yml` contains:

```yaml
version: 0.0.1-alpha.2
urls:
  docs: https://github.com/vincentarelbundock/calepin
```

The syntax `{{< var version >}}` inserts the value.

* *Calepin* version: {{< var version >}}
* Documentation URL: {{< var urls.docs >}}

## include

Include the content of another file. YAML front matter in the included file is stripped automatically:

```
{{{< include basics.qmd >}}}
```

The included content is expanded before block parsing, so code chunks, fenced divs, and other block structures in the included file are processed normally.

## video

Embed a video or streaming service. Supports YouTube, Vimeo, and direct video URLs:

```
{{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ >}}}
{{{< video https://vimeo.com/123456 width="640" height="480" title="My Video" >}}}
```

YouTube and Vimeo URLs are automatically converted to embed URLs.

## brand

Access branding information from `_brand.yml`:

- `{{< brand color primary >}}` --- returns a color value
- `{{< brand logo mark >}}` --- returns format-appropriate logo markup


# Plugin shortcodes

Custom shortcodes are implemented as plugins with a `shortcode` capability. Create a plugin directory with a `plugin.yml` manifest and an executable script.

## Example: greeting plugin

`_calepin/plugins/greeting/plugin.yml`:

```yaml
name: greeting
version: 0.1.0
description: "A friendly greeting shortcode"

provides:
  shortcode:
    run: shortcode.py
    names: [greeting]
```

`_calepin/plugins/greeting/shortcode.py`:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json, sys

data = json.load(sys.stdin)
name = data["args"][0] if data["args"] else "World"
fmt = data["format"]

if fmt == "html":
    print(f'<strong>Hello, {name}!</strong>')
elif fmt == "tex":
    print(f'\\textbf{{Hello, {name}!}}')
elif fmt == "typ":
    print(f'Hello, {name}!')
else:
    print(f'Hello, {name}!')
```

## Input protocol

The executable receives JSON on stdin:

```json
{
  "name": "greeting",
  "args": ["Alice"],
  "kwargs": {},
  "format": "html",
  "meta": {
    "title": "My Document",
    "author": ["Author Name"],
    "date": "2025-01-01"
  }
}
```

## Output protocol

Print the replacement text to stdout. The output is inserted directly into the document at the shortcode location.

- Exit 0: stdout replaces the shortcode.
- Exit non-zero: a warning is printed, and the shortcode is left as-is.

See [Plugins](plugins.qmd) for the full plugin system documentation, including how to scaffold a plugin with `calepin plugin init`.


# Resolution order

For each shortcode, *Calepin* checks:

1. Built-in shortcodes (`pagebreak`, `meta`, `env`, `var`, `include`, `video`, `brand`)
2. Plugin registry (in front matter order)

Built-in shortcodes take precedence. You cannot override them with plugins.
